tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low urine specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), suggesting significant renal dysfunction, possibly with tubular involvement. HbA1c is low, arguing against chronic hyperglycemia. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation for possible acute or chronic kidney disease and further assessment of underlying etiology.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
